he_046954.description: potassium-aggravated myotonia, presenting with Elevated serum creatine kinase, Muscle stiffness, Myotonia, and Stridor. Notably absent: Muscle weakness. Often co-occurs with myotonia fluctuans and myotonia permanens.

he_047021.description: glycogen storage disease, presenting with Hypercholesterolemia, X-linked recessive inheritance, Hepatomegaly, and Hypoglycemia. Notably absent: Hyperuricemia, Lactic acidosis, and Hypothyroidism. Treatment includes Penicillamine, Tiopronin, and Alglucosidase alfa. Contraindicated: Allantoin and Phenazopyridine. Often co-occurs with glycogen storage disease due to GLUT2 deficiency and glycogen storage disease due to glucose-6-phosphatase deficiency.
entities: glycogen storage disease due to GLUT2 deficiency
glycogen storage disease due to glucose-6-phosphatase deficiency

he_047046.description: glucocorticoid deficiency, presenting with Failure to thrive, Hyperkalemia, Seizure, and Hyponatremia. Notably absent: Abnormal circulating renin and Abnormal circulating aldosterone. Often co-occurs with familial glucocorticoid deficiency.

he_047055.description: long QT syndrome, presenting with Prolonged QT interval, Syncope, Autosomal dominant inheritance, and Bradycardia. Notably absent: Hearing abnormality. Contraindicated: Ondansetron and Ranolazine. Risk factors: Arsenic. Often co-occurs with Jervell-Lange Nielsen syndrome and Jervell and Lange-Nielsen syndrome.

he_047075.description: maturity-onset diabetes of the young, presenting with Infantile onset, Hyperglycemia, Type II diabetes mellitus, and Autosomal dominant inheritance. Notably absent: Abnormality of body mass index, Diabetic ketoacidosis, and Autoimmune antibody positivity. Often co-occurs with renal cysts and diabetes syndrome and Fanconi renotubular syndrome 4 with maturity-onset diabetes of the young.
entities: Fanconi renotubular syndrome 4 with maturity-onset diabetes of the young

he_047169.description: coronary artery disease, autosomal dominant, presenting with Hypercholesterolemia, Chest pain, Autosomal dominant inheritance, and Osteoporosis. Notably absent: Abnormality of cardiovascular system morphology, Abnormal heart morphology, and Type I diabetes mellitus. Often co-occurs with Mendelian disease and coronary artery disease.

he_047173.description: familial pseudohyperkalemia, presenting with Hand tremor, Generalized muscle weakness, Hyperkalemia, and Muscle spasm. Notably absent: Hemolytic anemia. Often co-occurs with hereditary stomatocytosis.

he_047199.description: Wolfram syndrome, presenting with Seizure, Pigmentary retinopathy, Ataxia, and Sideroblastic anemia. Notably absent: Diabetes insipidus. Often co-occurs with Wolfram syndrome, mitochondrial form and syndromic genetic deafness.

he_047207.description: renal tubular acidosis, presenting with Increased red cell osmotic resistance, Band keratopathy, Autosomal recessive inheritance, and Hyperchloremic acidosis. Notably absent: Hypercalciuria. Treatment includes Potassium citrate. Contraindicated: Dichlorophen and Arginine. Often co-occurs with osteopetrosis and renal tubular acidosis, distal, with or without sensorineural hearing loss.
entities: renal tubular acidosis, distal, with or without sensorineural hearing loss

he_047223.description: cystoid macular edema, presenting with Strabismus, Pericentral retinitis pigmentosa, Hypermetropia, and Edema. Notably absent: Abnormal electroretinogram. Contraindicated: Glimepiride and Metformin. Often co-occurs with macular retinal edema and unclassified primitive or secondary maculopathy.

he_047232.description: catecholaminergic polymorphic ventricular tachycardia, presenting with Bradycardia, Seizure, Autosomal recessive inheritance, and Syncope. Notably absent: Prolonged QT interval. Contraindicated: Synephrine and Guaifenesin. Often co-occurs with long QT syndrome and polymorphic ventricular tachycardia.

he_047359.description: hereditary spastic paraplegia, presenting with Difficulty walking, Hyperactive patellar reflex, Inability to walk, and Polyneuropathy. Notably absent: Fasciculations, Seizure, and Abnormality of the cerebrospinal fluid. Often co-occurs with Charcot-Marie-Tooth disease and pure hereditary spastic paraplegia.

he_047384.description: familial long QT syndrome, presenting with Prolonged QTc interval, Syncope, Sinus bradycardia, and Abnormal T-wave. Notably absent: Hearing impairment. Contraindicated: Ondansetron and Ranolazine. Often co-occurs with Andersen-Tawil syndrome and Jervell and Lange-Nielsen syndrome.

he_047413.description: pulmonary venoocclusive disease, presenting with Pulmonary venous occlusion, Centrilobular ground-glass opacification on pulmonary HRCT, Pulmonary arterial hypertension, and Elevated jugular venous pressure. Notably absent: Antinuclear antibody positivity and Cytoplasmic antineutrophil antibody positivity. Treatment includes Propranolol. Contraindicated: Ambrisentan and Bosentan. Often co-occurs with pulmonary veno-occlusive disease and/or pulmonary capillary haemangiomatosis and hypertension.
entities: Centrilobular ground-glass opacification on pulmonary HRCT
pulmonary veno-occlusive disease and/or pulmonary capillary haemangiomatosis

he_047414.description: late-onset isolated ACTH deficiency, presenting with Decreased circulating cortisol level, Adrenocorticotropin deficient adrenal insufficiency, Lethargy, and Muscle weakness. Notably absent: Hyperkalemia. Often co-occurs with acquired pituitary hormone deficiency.

he_047415.description: congenital isolated adrenocorticotropic hormone deficiency (disease), presenting with Cholestasis, Decreased circulating cortisol level, Seizure, and Infantile onset. Notably absent: Hyperkalemia. Often co-occurs with hypopituitarism and non-acquired pituitary hormone deficiency.

he_047432.description: incessant infant ventricular tachycardia, presenting with Ventricular tachycardia, Congestive heart failure, Cardiac arrest, and Prolonged QRS complex. Notably absent: Left ventricular systolic dysfunction. Contraindicated: Synephrine and Guaifenesin. Often co-occurs with non-genetic cardiac rhythm disease.

he_047450.description: spastic ataxia, presenting with Bilateral tonic-clonic seizure, Skeletal muscle atrophy, Dysphagia, and Progressive. Notably absent: Seizure, Abnormality of the cerebrospinal fluid, and Generalized amyotrophy. Often co-occurs with spasticity-ataxia-gait anomalies syndrome and autosomal dominant spastic ataxia.

he_047480.description: Tako-tsubo cardiomyopathy, presenting with Mildly reduced ejection fraction, T-wave inversion, ST segment elevation, and Chest pain. Notably absent: Coronary artery atherosclerosis, Coronary artery stenosis, and Abnormal coronary artery morphology. Often co-occurs with unclassified cardiomyopathy.

he_047495.anchor: deficiency in anterior pituitary function - variable immunodeficiency syndrome
he_047495.description: deficiency in anterior pituitary function - variable immunodeficiency syndrome, presenting with Recurrent otitis media, Diplopia, Global developmental delay, and Decreased response to growth hormone stimuation test. Notably absent: Vitiligo, Hyperkalemia, and Autoimmune antibody positivity. Often co-occurs with disease associated with non-acquired combined pituitary hormone deficiency and immunodeficiency, common variable.
entities: deficiency in anterior pituitary function - variable immunodeficiency syndrome
Decreased response to growth hormone stimuation test
disease associated with non-acquired combined pituitary hormone deficiency

he_047496.anchor: rapid-onset childhood obesity-hypothalamic dysfunction-hypoventilation-autonomic dysregulation syndrome
he_047496.description: rapid-onset childhood obesity-hypothalamic dysfunction-hypoventilation-autonomic dysregulation syndrome, presenting with Macrocephaly, Anteverted nares, Decreased lacrimation, and Emotional lability. Notably absent: Anxiety and Hyperkalemia. Often co-occurs with syndromic genetic obesity.
entities: rapid-onset childhood obesity-hypothalamic dysfunction-hypoventilation-autonomic dysregulation syndrome

he_047565.description: myotonia fluctuans, presenting with Muscle stiffness, EMG: myotonic discharges, Spasticity of facial muscles, and Exercise-induced muscle stiffness. Notably absent: Cold-sensitive myotonia. Often co-occurs with potassium-aggravated myotonia.

he_047677.description: familial atrial fibrillation, presenting with Autosomal dominant inheritance, Permanent atrial fibrillation, Bradycardia, and Palpitations. Often co-occurs with atrial fibrillation (disease) and genetic cardiac rhythm disease.

he_047771.description: hyperkalemic periodic paralysis, presenting with Episodic flaccid weakness, Myotonia, Hyperkalemia, and Infantile onset. Contraindicated: Guaifenesin and Guaiacol. Often co-occurs with familial periodic paralysis.

he_047781.description: hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, presenting with Hypertrophic cardiomyopathy, Autosomal dominant inheritance, Dyspnea, and Congestive heart failure. Treatment includes Propranolol.
